nodes.name: initialize_message_passing_state
nodes.description: Builds the immutable loopy-belief-propagation state object from the PGM, including message tables/buffers and iteration counter. Acts as a memoization cache (msg/msg_new) to prevent infinite recursive cycles during message passing on cyclic graphs by storing and reusing previously computed messages.

inputs.name: pgm
inputs.constraints: Factor graph structure with variable/factor neighborhoods and factor tables.

outputs.name: state_out
outputs.constraints: New object; includes initialized factor->variable and variable->factor message storage plus t=0.

nodes.conceptual_summary: immutable_bidirectional_edge_state_initializer. Constructs an initial immutable state container for iterative message exchange on a bipartite dependency graph. For every directed edge in both directions, it allocates/initializes aligned buffer tables with shapes derived from endpoint neighborhood structure and local compatibility tensors. It returns this fully materialized state together with an iteration index set to zero, enabling deterministic first-step updates.. Applications: Initializing flux-exchange buffers on reaction-network bipartite graphs before iterative mass-balance reconciliation in systems chemistry, Bootstrapping node-edge signal tables for iterative parity-consistency decoding in digital communication error-correction graphs, Preallocating bidirectional influence tensors for iterative constraint relaxation in multi-body robotic kinematic assemblies, Setting up directional update caches for iterative resource-flow equilibration in power-grid topology optimization

nodes.name: run_loopy_message_passing_and_belief_query
nodes.description: Runs pure loopy message-passing iterations (variable->factor and factor->variable updates with normalization) and returns the queried variable belief with an updated immutable state.

inputs.name: state_in
inputs.constraints: Immutable input snapshot; msg/msg_new/t are read and transformed into a new state.

inputs.name: v_name
inputs.constraints: Must be a valid variable name in pgm.

outputs.name: belief
outputs.constraints: Probability vector for v_name; sums to 1.

outputs.name: state_out
outputs.constraints: New object with updated msg and t after iterations; no hidden mutation.

nodes.type_signature: (state_in: BPStateModelSpec{pgm, msg, msg_new, t}, v_name: string, num_iter: int) -> tuple[NormalizedVector, BPStateModelSpec{pgm, msg, msg_new, t}]
nodes.conceptual_summary: Iterative Bipartite Message Equilibration and Local Marginal Extraction. Applies repeated local update rules on a cyclic bipartite dependency graph, alternating directed messages from node set A to node set B and back. Each message is reweighted by neighboring incoming messages and then normalized so every edge-wise message lies on a simplex, driving the system toward a fixed-point approximation of global consistency. After a specified number of iterations, it extracts the queried node's normalized marginal weight vector and returns it with a newly constructed state snapshot.. Applications: Iterative decoding of sparse parity-check communication codes via constraint-variable exchanges, Multi-sensor occupancy inference on discretized spatial maps with cyclic neighborhood constraints, Distributed fault localization in infrastructure networks by propagating consistency signals between component and constraint nodes, Inference of discrete conformational states in interaction networks where pairwise and higher-order compatibility rules are known

nodes.name: validate_iteration_request
nodes.description: Checks that the requested iteration count is not behind the current state (`state_in.t <= num_iter`) and emits the validated target iteration.

outputs.name: target_iter
outputs.constraints: Defined only when `state_in.t <= num_iter`; otherwise this step yields an invalid-request error.

nodes.type_signature: (state_in: BPStateModelSpec{pgm, msg, msg_new, t}, num_iter: int) -> int

nodes.name: compute_variable_to_factor_messages
nodes.description: For each edge, computes the variable-to-factor message as the product of all incoming factor messages except the target factor, then normalizes.

inputs.name: state_in
inputs.constraints: Graph must provide factor/variable typing, neighbors, factor tables, and variable ranks.

outputs.name: msg_new
outputs.constraints: Contains updated variable-to-factor messages; factor-to-variable entries are unchanged.

nodes.type_signature: (state_in: BPStateModelSpec{pgm, msg, msg_new, t}, target_iter: int) -> MessageTable

nodes.name: compute_factor_to_variable_messages
nodes.description: For each edge, computes the factor-to-variable message by multiplying the factor potential with all incoming variable messages except the target variable, marginalizing out non-target variables via np.sum(axis=...), then normalizes.

inputs.name: state_in
inputs.constraints: Graph must provide factor/variable typing, neighbors, factor tables, and variable ranks.

inputs.name: msg_new
inputs.constraints: Must contain the updated variable-to-factor messages from the current iteration.

outputs.name: msg_new
outputs.constraints: Contains updated variable-to-factor and factor-to-variable messages for the current iteration.

nodes.type_signature: (state_in: BPStateModelSpec{pgm, msg, msg_new, t}, msg_new: MessageTable) -> MessageTable

nodes.name: commit_messages_and_advance_iteration
nodes.description: Copies msg_new into msg and increments the iteration counter t, producing an updated immutable state snapshot.

inputs.name: msg_new
inputs.constraints: Must contain all updated messages for the current iteration.

outputs.name: state_out
outputs.constraints: `state_out.t == state_in.t + 1`; `msg` contains latest normalized directional messages.

nodes.type_signature: (state_in: BPStateModelSpec{pgm, msg, msg_new, t}, msg_new: MessageTable) -> BPStateModelSpec{pgm, msg, msg_new, t}

nodes.name: collect_incoming_factor_messages_for_query_variable
nodes.description: Traverses the query variable's neighboring factors and collects each factor->variable message from the updated message table.

inputs.name: state_out
inputs.constraints: Must already be advanced to requested iteration.

inputs.name: v_name
inputs.constraints: Must be a variable node in the factor graph.

outputs.name: incoming_messages
outputs.constraints: One message per neighboring factor of `v_name`.

nodes.type_signature: (state_out: BPStateModelSpec{pgm, msg, msg_new, t}, v_name: string) -> List[FactorMessage]

nodes.name: compose_joint_query_distribution
nodes.description: Combines all incoming factor->variable messages into a single unnormalized belief factor using joint multiplication. Fuses incoming messages via numpy broadcasting and np.sum(axis=...) to compute the marginal distribution.

inputs.name: incoming_messages
inputs.constraints: All messages must be over the query variable domain.

nodes.name: normalize_joint_distribution_to_belief
nodes.description: Normalizes the combined query distribution so probabilities sum to 1, yielding the queried variable belief.
